risk_if_not_implemented: Without Automated Records Management & Review, residual security, compliance, or resilience risk may accumulate, potentially leading to control failures or non-compliance.

compensating_control_1.name: Physical Access Control
compensating_control_1.description: Physical access control mechanisms exist to enforce physical access authorizations for all physical access points (including designated entry/exit points) to facilities (excluding those areas within the facility officially designated as publicly accessible).
compensating_control_1.justification: Physical Access Control (PES-03) provides access control enforcement that compensates for the absence of Automated Records Management & Review (PES-06.4) by restricting system and data access to authorized users through alternative identity and access mechanisms. Given the technology-focused nature of this control, this compensating control targets the same underlying risk objective through an alternative approach, helping to maintain an acceptable level of residual risk until the primary control can be implemented.

compensating_control_2.name: Visitor Control
compensating_control_2.description: Physical access control mechanisms exist to identify, authorize and monitor visitors before allowing access to the facility (other than areas designated as publicly accessible).
compensating_control_2.justification: Visitor Control (PES-06) provides physical access control that compensates for the absence of Automated Records Management & Review (PES-06.4) by preventing unauthorized physical interaction with systems and infrastructure. Given the technology-focused nature of this control, this compensating control targets the same underlying risk objective through an alternative approach, helping to maintain an acceptable level of residual risk until the primary control can be implemented.
